title: Xtra Fresh Mexican Pork Cochinita Pibil
description: A fresh and fluffy one-pot Mexican dish featuring free-range egg and pasta-water-emulsion, macrobiotic-friendly, in the form of cochinita pibil. This recipe combines the flavors of Mexico with a unique twist.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the beaten eggs, pasta water emulsion, orange juice, lime juice, olive oil, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper.
Add the pork shoulder to the bowl and marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Remove the pork from the marinade and place it in a large Dutch oven.
Pour the marinade over the pork and cover the Dutch oven with a lid.
Transfer the Dutch oven to the preheated oven and braise the pork for 2 hours.
After 2 hours, remove the lid and continue to braise for an additional 30 minutes.
Remove the pork from the oven and let it rest for 10 minutes before slicing.
Slice the pork into thin strips and serve with the juices from the Dutch oven.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
